reason: Adding the 'nodev' mount option to /dev/shm is a standard security hardening practice to prevent the creation of device files in a shared memory directory. It is a non-disruptive change to /etc/fstab that does not affect system boot or core authentication services.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential for rare application failure if an application specifically requires creating device files in /dev/shm (highly non-standard)
